description:
Operating Area: Croatia

Indulge in the ultimate luxury experience aboard the Lagoon 77, a sophisticated catamaran crafted for those who crave the perfect fusion of elegance, comfort, and adventure. At 77 feet, this magnificent yacht offers spacious living areas, world-class amenities, and an exceptional crew of four, ensuring your every need is met with impeccable service.

The master cabin is a sanctuary of luxury, featuring a plush double bed, en-suite bathroom, and a private teak balcony with swimming ladder - accessible via a hydraulic opening hull door. Enjoy stunning views from your private balcony, sip a cocktail as you watch the sunset, or relax with a book as the sound of the sea serenades you.

The Lagoon 77 boasts four beautifully appointed guest cabins, including three doubles and one convertible twin cabin, each with en-suite bathrooms and premium furnishings. The expansive flybridge and sunbathing area provide ample space to relax and take in the surroundings, while the hydraulic platform and gangway make it easy to access the crystal-clear waters below.

Equipped with a Highfield tender, Seabob, paddle boards, kayak, and a full range of snorkeling gear, the Lagoon 77 offers endless opportunities for water-based adventure, allowing you to explore the pristine seas at your own pace.

The name "Adriatic Dragon" blends the mystique of the Adriatic Sea with the power and allure of the dragon. "Adriatic" evokes the beauty and tranquility of the Mediterranean, while "Dragon" symbolizes strength, adventure, and untamed freedom—perfectly capturing the spirit of a yacht built for both elegance and daring exploration on the open water.

blueprint.name: ADRIATIC DRAGON
tenders: Highfield OM Deluxe 500 with outboard engine Honda 100 hp

crew.name: Nikša Gjurašić
crew.bio:
Born in the historic city of Dubrovnik, renowned for its rich maritime heritage, Niko has always been deeply inspired by the sea. Growing up near a local marina, he developed a passion for boats from an early age, learning about them as soon as he could walk. For six years, Niko was an active member of the Neptun Rowing Club, competing in numerous national and international regattas with notable success, eventually becoming a member of the national team.

Niko is also a proud Canadian resident, blending his Croatian heritage with a global outlook shaped by diverse international experiences. Highly motivated, hardworking, and reliable, he is recognized for his adaptability and his ability to excel in dynamic, ever-changing environments. A strong communicator across all age groups, he possesses excellent leadership skills. With more than 36 years of experience across the Croatian Navy, Merchant Navy, and yacht industry, Niko has sailed all the world's oceans and navigated most seas, demonstrating an unwavering dedication to self-discipline, professionalism, and a strong work ethic. He has been the captain of Adriatic Dragon since 2020.

Skills/Expertise/Languages:
-	Master of a ship up to 3000 GT by the STCW regulation II/2
-	Advanced Open Water Diver, SSI
-	Skipper instructor
-	Basic engineering, mechanics - Fluent in English, Italian and Croatian

Personal Interests:
A lifelong learner, he continuously seeks out opportunities to expand knowledge and experiences. In his personal time, Niko enjoys a wide range of activities, such as cooking, sailing, swimming, weight training, photography, and hiking.

crew.name: Jadran Božić
crew.bio:
Jadran Božić grew up in Split, Croatia, and has always had a love for sailing. He earned his Yachtmaster certification at 19 and has been working on yachts ever since, accumulating over 20 years of experience. Jadran is known for his exceptional attention to detail and care for safety of guests onboard, outstanding navigational skills, and unwavering composure under pressure.

Skills/Expertise/Languages:
- Yachtmaster 100 GT
- Fluent in English and Croatian

Personal Interests:
Jadran loves spending time at sea, which he describes as calming environment allowing one to introspect and really enjoy the simple things in life. 

The Motivation Behind Profession:
Being at sea allows me to experience the calmness and beauty of it, and the opportunity to share that feeling with our guests makes me proud of what I do. For me, this is more than just a job, it’s a truly unforgettable journey. Every day, I bring the same passion and dedication as I did when I first started.

crew.name: Tihomir Krklec
crew.bio:
Tihomir grew up in the capital city of Zagreb, where he attended an art high school and later studied to become a graphic designer. Throughout his education and career, he always had a deep passion for food, influenced by his family, who were great gourmets and excellent cooks. After 13 years of working in the art scene, he applied for MasterChef Croatia and made it to the final week. From that day on, everything revolved around cooking. He has since been involved in several very successful projects, launched a few restaurants, organized food festivals, filmed food-related TV shows, and became a private chef on yachts and in private homes.

Skills/Expertise/Languages:
-	Strong focus on learning about food, the latest techniques in gastronomy, zero-waste culture, and exploring traditional Croatian cuisine in modern way.
-	Completed numerous courses and participated in collaborative cooking with some of Croatia’s top chefs.
-	Attended several workshops on wine and winemaking.
-  Highly open-minded, fun to communicate and socialize with, and excellent in teamwork.
-  Fluent in English.

Personal Interests:
When Tihomir's not on the yacht, he organizes various food events, consults for restaurants, and creates new seasonal menus. He also provides private cooking for clients and continues to educate himself in gastronomy. Alongside this, he runs an art studio with his team, where they work on new paintings, exhibitions, and future projects. In his free time, he is deeply passionate about photography and traveling.

crew.name: Martina Ivanković
crew.bio:
Born in Zagreb and having lived in various parts of Croatia, Martina discovered her passion for sailing the moment she first stepped aboard a yacht. With a wealth of experience in hospitality, tourism, and design, she has earned a reputation for providing exceptional service. By combining her strong hospitality background with a love for yachting and design, Martina brings a unique skill set that elevates every guest experience.

Certifications & Licenses:
- D2 certificate with STCW VI/1 (First Aid & CPR, Personal survival techniques fire, prevention and fire 
fighting, Personal safety and social responsibility, operating VHF system)
-	Valid seafarer's book
-	ENG1 medical certificate
-	Certifications in HABC L2 Food & Hygiene
-	Croatian skipper's license B
-	Masters degree in Costume design
-	Interior Design Diploma
-	Certified Tour guide (Dubrovnik - Neretva county)
-	Certified Hiking guide

Skills/Expertise/Languages:
-	Exceptional customer and silver service skills
-	Experience in fine dining cuisine preparation and presentation
-	Mixology enthusiast 
-	Fluent in English and Croatian
